chunks.title: Introduction to Cravings and Diet
chunks.summary: The segment begins with a question from a viewer about managing carbohydrate cravings while on a carnivore diet. The discussion opens up the topic of nutrient deficiencies and the importance of including organ meats for optimal health.
chunks.transcript: next question is from patty who says she's been carnivore for eight months how do i get rid of my carbohydrate cravings i still have a sweet tooth to which i would ask patty are you drinking coffee are you getting organs in your diet so let's start with the organs muscle meat is great it does not have all of the nutrients that humans really need to thrive this is why i built heart and soil it's why we make desiccated organs i want you guys to get fresh organs if you can but you can't please consider getting desiccated organs you can check us out at heart and soil dot co dot co

chunks.title: Importance of Organ Meats
chunks.summary: The speaker emphasizes the nutritional benefits of organ meats over muscle meat, detailing specific nutrients found in organs such as liver that are essential for optimal human health. He advocates for consuming organ meats as part of a balanced animal-based diet.
chunks.transcript: and start with beef organs that's the simplest one six capsules a day we'll get you some organs patty that is probably what you need and you need to get the nutrients in those organs what nutrients am i talking about liver for instance has more vitamin a more choline more biotin more riboflavin more folate more selenium more zinc more copper it's all complementary nutrients to the muscle meat muscle meat's great doesn't have everything heart coenzyme q10 other peptides that are valuable testicle for instance valuable for most men and women hormonal precursors all sorts of valuable things ergothioneine in the liver these are essential nutrients peptides cofactors hormonal precursors even hormones and these things help humans become optimal every culture in the world eats nose to tail why humans aren't doing it that's the problem and i think the nutrient

chunks.title: Influence of Caffeine on Nutrient Absorption
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to the effect of caffeine, particularly from coffee, on mineral loss and overall health. The speaker cites studies linking caffeine consumption to accelerated bone loss and other negative health impacts.
chunks.transcript: deficiencies that can come with only eating muscle meat will worsen carbohydrate cravings the second question i have for patty like i said are you drinking coffee we know that caffeine will cause mineral loss there are many studies like this one caffeine and bone loss and healthy post-menopausal women this study shows that daily consumption of caffeine in amounts equal to or greater than that obtained from two to three servings of brewed coffee may accelerate bone loss from the spine and total body in women if calcium intakes below the recommended dairy dietary allowance of 800 milligrams we know that caffeine is going to cause mineral loss if it's causing calcium loss it's also causing magnesium loss and manganese and selenium coffee is guys i've said it i've said it over in the past i'll say it again if you are drinking coffee you are hindering your optimal progress look if that is the last vice that you have so be it but i still think it's i don't think it's doing you any good i think it is jacking your hormones i think it is spiking your cortisol i think it is activating your sympathetic nervous system you have not looked very far on any social media platform or youtube to find testimonials of people who feel so much better without caffeine and coffee

chunks.title: Consequences of Caffeine Consumption
chunks.summary: The speaker argues that drinking coffee may hinder progress on a carnivore diet, suggesting that it may spike cortisol levels and negatively impact hormonal balance. He encourages viewers to consider eliminating caffeine for better health outcomes.
chunks.transcript: it is borrowing tomorrow's happiness today i'm pretty sure patty's drinking coffee and that is worsening your mineral status overall plus potentially a lack of organs and that leads to carbohydrate cravings in my opinion if you do those things i think you will feel much better what else can lead to mineral loss vegetables lectins fiber presumably patty has eliminating vegetables because she's been on a carnivore diet for eight months but my suspicion is coffee and lack of organs are potentially to blame there so let me know how that stuff works for you guys i do get that

chunks.title: Other Factors Affecting Nutritional Deficiencies
chunks.summary: Aside from caffeine, the speaker discusses how vegetables, lectins, and fiber might also contribute to mineral loss, especially in someone adhering to a carnivore diet. The conversation reinforces the significance of ensuring adequate nutrient intake.
chunks.transcript: question a lot what do i do about carbohydrate cravings make sure you're not drinking coffee make sure you're getting plenty of nutrients and the last point i will add there is fruit is fine honey is fine you can incorporate those in your life they may help you transition and you'll be fine but i think the carbohydrate carvings go beyond that for most people and it probably is nutrient deficiency and or

chunks.title: Managing Carbohydrate Cravings
chunks.summary: The speaker wraps up by discussing practical tips for managing carbohydrate cravings, including the suggestion to consume fruits and honey as transitional foods while emphasizing the need to address nutrient deficiencies for long-term success.
